# pipe — Vision Analysis Pipeline

Train a CIFAR-10 CNN, capture every misclassification with its distortion type,
cluster failures in embedding space, and generate a deep AI reasoning report —
all from **one command**.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Directory Structure](#directory-structure)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [How the Pipeline Works](#how-the-pipeline-works)
4. [Version Comparison](#version-comparison)
5. [Running the Pipeline](#running-the-pipeline)
6. [VLM Server Setup](#vlm-server-setup)
7. [All Flags](#all-flags)
8. [Report Structure](#report-structure)
9. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)

---

## Directory Structure

```
pipe/
├── __main__.py                        # enables: python3 -m pipe --version v3
├── test.py                            # main entry point — orchestrates all 4 steps
│
├── debug_logger.py                    # Lightning callback: logs metrics + runs YOLO distortion classifier
├── export_misclassified.py            # extracts misclassified samples from a training log JSON
├── distortion_diagnostic_report.py   # PCA → t-SNE clustering + archetype selection
│
├── best.pt                            # YOLO distortion classifier weights (blur/jpeg/pixelate/noise)
├── data/                              # CIFAR-10 dataset (auto-downloaded on first run)
├── logs/                              # training_log_*.json · misclassified_*.json · *.png plots
├── reports/                           # distortion_report.json · distortion_clusters.png
│
├── v1/
│   ├── vision_reasoning_report.py    # single-turn per-image VLM analysis
│   └── help.md
│
├── v2/
│   ├── vision_reasoning_report_v2.py # multi-image batching + two-turn reasoning
│   └── help_v2.md
│
└── v3/
    ├── vision_reasoning_report_v3.py  # entry point: RAG + LangGraph + Pydantic
    ├── config.py                      # constants, default paths, LLM settings
    ├── data_loader.py                 # JSON log loading utilities
    ├── image_sampler.py               # sampling + semantic deduplication
    ├── embeddings.py                  # pixel embeddings + cosine similarity search
    ├── rag.py                         # RAGStore: cross-run vector index (rag_index.npz)
    ├── schemas.py                     # Pydantic models for structured LLM output
    ├── tools.py                       # agent stat verification tools
    ├── chains.py                      # LLM factory + prompt builders
    ├── graph.py                       # LangGraph: observe → hypothesise → verify → conclude
    ├── renderer.py                    # Markdown report writer + dynamic recommendations
    ├── server_utils.py                # vLLM health polling + wait logic
    ├── help_v3.md
    └── __init__.py
```

---

## Quick Start

### With VLM (full AI report)

Open **two terminals**:

**Terminal 1 — start the VLM server (keep running)**
```bash
cd ~/vision_dev_project
source venv_vision/bin/activate

VLLM_USE_V1=0 HUGGINGFACE_HUB_CACHE="/mnt/data/pratik_models" \
python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct \
  --quantization bitsandbytes \
  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.4 \
  --max-model-len 4096 \
  --enforce-eager \
  --port 8000
```
Wait until you see: `INFO:     Application startup complete.`

**Terminal 2 — run the full pipeline**
```bash
cd ~/vision_dev_project
source venv_vision/bin/activate
python3 -m pipe --version v3
```
Output → `ai_reasoning_summary_v3.md` in the project root.

### Without VLM (stats + clusters only, no GPU needed)

```bash
cd ~/vision_dev_project
source venv_vision/bin/activate
python3 -m pipe --version v3 --no-vlm
```

---

## How the Pipeline Works

Every version runs the same **4 steps** in sequence.
`test.py` handles all of them automatically — you never need to run them manually.

```
Step 1  test.py  (training)
        Trains SimpleCNN on CIFAR-10 for 3 epochs.
        debug_logger.py callback records every misclassified image,
        runs the YOLO distortion model (best.pt) on each one, and
        saves per-epoch metrics.
        Output → logs/training_log_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json

Step 2  export_misclassified.py
        Extracts the misclassified samples from the training log
        into a standalone JSON file.
        Output → logs/misclassified_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json

Step 3  distortion_diagnostic_report.py
        Decodes every misclassified image, extracts ResNet18 features,
        runs PCA (50 components) → t-SNE (2D), and selects:
          • 3 "typical" images (closest to cluster centroid)
          • 3 "outlier" images (furthest from centroid)
        per distortion type as the archetypes the VLM will inspect.
        Output → reports/distortion_report.json
               → reports/distortion_clusters.png

Step 4  v[1|2|3]/vision_reasoning_report_*.py
        Sends representative images to Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct via vLLM.
        Produces a structured Markdown reasoning report with root causes,
        blind spot summary, and recommendations.
        Output → ai_reasoning_summary[_v2|_v3].md  (project root)
```

### Sample outputs from Step 3

| Distortion distribution (Step 2) | t-SNE cluster map (Step 3) |
|---|---|
| ![Distortion distribution](data:image/png;base64,) | ![t-SNE clusters](data:image/png;base64,) |
| Bar chart — failures per distortion type | ★ = typical centroid · ✕ = outlier |

Training-time PCA view (logged live during Step 1):

![Training-time PCA cluster](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## Version Comparison

| Feature | V1 | V2 | V3 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Images per VLM call | 1 per image | All samples for type | All samples (deduped) |
| Turns per distortion | 1 | 2 | 2 |
| Labels in Turn 1 | Shown | Hidden | Hidden |
| Cross-image analysis | No | Yes | Yes |
| Semantic deduplication | No | No | Yes (cosine > 0.96 removed) |
| RAG — cross-run memory | No | No | Yes (`rag_index.npz`) |
| Agentic verify loop | No | No | Yes (LangGraph) |
| Pydantic structured output | No | No | Yes |
| Dynamic recommendations | No | No | Yes (LLM-generated) |
| VLM calls (default) | 12 | 8 | 8–16 |
| Output file | `ai_reasoning_summary.md` | `ai_reasoning_summary_v2.md` | `ai_reasoning_summary_v3.md` |

### V1 — single-turn per-image

Sends each image individually with full metadata (true label, predicted label,
distortion type, confidence). One prompt → one response per image. The blind
spot summary is assembled by joining individual root causes — not synthesised
by the model.

**Best for:** quick inspection, minimal server load, debugging a single image.

### V2 — multi-image batching + two-turn reasoning

Batches all sampled images for a distortion type into one call.
- **Turn 1:** Labels withheld — model describes only what it sees visually.
- **Turn 2:** Labels revealed — model synthesises a cross-image analysis
  (shared failure pattern, typical vs outlier contrast, root cause).

**Best for:** standard analysis runs once the VLM server is warm.

### V3 — RAG + LangGraph agent + Pydantic + dynamic recommendations

Builds on V2 with five major upgrades:

1. **Semantic deduplication** — removes near-duplicate images (cosine similarity
   > 0.96) before sending to the VLM, ensuring maximally diverse batches.

2. **RAG (cross-run memory)** — encodes every past misclassified image into a
   vector index (`reports/rag_index.npz`). Retrieves the 5 most visually similar
   historical failures and injects them into Turn 2 so the model can distinguish
   novel vs recurring failure patterns.

3. **LangGraph agentic loop** — 4-node state machine:
   `observe → hypothesise → verify → conclude`.
   The `verify` node calls stat tools (confusion count, epoch trend, top
   confusion pairs) to cross-check the hypothesis against real data. If the
   data contradicts the hypothesis, the graph loops back to `hypothesise`
   (up to 2 iterations).

4. **Pydantic structured output** — the `Turn2Analysis` schema enforces
   JSON-compliant responses with field-level validation, making the report
   deterministic and parseable.

5. **Dynamic recommendations** — Section 6 is generated live by the LLM
   from the current run's failure distribution and root causes — not hardcoded.

**Best for:** deep diagnostic runs, multi-run trend analysis, production debugging.

---

## Running the Pipeline

### Recommended — package entry point (from project root)

```bash
cd ~/vision_dev_project
source venv_vision/bin/activate

python3 -m pipe                        # training only
python3 -m pipe --version v1           # train → v1 report
python3 -m pipe --version v2           # train → v2 report
python3 -m pipe --version v3           # train → v3 report (needs VLM server)
python3 -m pipe --version v3 --no-vlm  # train → v3 report (stats only, no GPU)
```

### Alternative — run test.py directly

```bash
cd ~/vision_dev_project/pipe
source ../venv_vision/bin/activate

python3 test.py                        # training only
python3 test.py --version v1
python3 test.py --version v2
python3 test.py --version v3
python3 test.py --version v3 --no-vlm
```

### Manual — run each step individually

Useful when you want to re-run a single step without re-training.

**Step 1 — Train**
```bash
cd ~/vision_dev_project/pipe
python3 test.py
# → logs/training_log_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json
```

**Step 2 — Export misclassified samples**
```bash
python3 export_misclassified.py \
  --input  logs/training_log_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json \
  --output logs/misclassified_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json
```

**Step 3 — Distortion diagnostic report**
```bash
python3 distortion_diagnostic_report.py \
  --base-dir logs/misclassified_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json \
  --output   reports/distortion_report.json \
  --plot     reports/distortion_clusters.png
```
Pass the `.json` file (not a folder) — images are decoded automatically.

**Step 4 — AI reasoning report**

V1:
```bash
PYTHONPATH=~/vision_dev_project python3 v1/vision_reasoning_report.py
# → ~/vision_dev_project/ai_reasoning_summary.md
```

V2:
```bash
PYTHONPATH=~/vision_dev_project python3 v2/vision_reasoning_report_v2.py
# → ~/vision_dev_project/ai_reasoning_summary_v2.md
```

V3:
```bash
PYTHONPATH=~/vision_dev_project python3 v3/vision_reasoning_report_v3.py
PYTHONPATH=~/vision_dev_project python3 v3/vision_reasoning_report_v3.py --no-vlm
PYTHONPATH=~/vision_dev_project python3 v3/vision_reasoning_report_v3.py --no-rag
# → ~/vision_dev_project/ai_reasoning_summary_v3.md
```

---

## VLM Server Setup

Required for V1, V2, V3 with VLM enabled. Not needed for `--no-vlm` mode.

```bash
cd ~/vision_dev_project
source venv_vision/bin/activate

VLLM_USE_V1=0 HUGGINGFACE_HUB_CACHE="/mnt/data/pratik_models" \
python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct \
  --quantization bitsandbytes \
  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.4 \
  --max-model-len 4096 \
  --enforce-eager \
  --port 8000
```

Wait for: `INFO:     Application startup complete.`

- **First run** downloads the model (~5.8 GB to `/mnt/data/pratik_models`).
- **Subsequent runs** load from cache in ~30 seconds.
- All report scripts poll `/health` every 5 s for up to 5 minutes and start
  automatically — you don't need to manually coordinate between terminals.

> **VRAM note:** Qwen2.5-VL at 4-bit uses ~5.8 GB.
> Lower `--gpu-memory-utilization` to `0.3` or reduce `--samples` to `2` if you hit OOM.
> Always use `--max-model-len 4096` for V2/V3 — the two-turn prompts need the extra headroom.

---

## All Flags

### `python3 -m pipe` / `test.py`

| Flag | Default | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `--version {v1,v2,v3}` | _(none)_ | Run the full pipeline with this report version. Omit for training only. |
| `--no-vlm` | false | Pass `--no-vlm` through to the report script (stats only, no server needed). |

### Shared flags — all report scripts (V1 / V2 / V3)

| Flag | Default | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `--logs-dir PATH` | `pipe/logs` | Folder containing training and misclassified JSON logs. |
| `--report PATH` | `pipe/reports/distortion_report.json` | Path to the distortion diagnostic report. |
| `--output PATH` | project root, version-named | Output Markdown report path. |
| `--samples N` | `3` | Images per distortion type sent to the VLM. |
| `--training-log PATH` | _(auto: latest)_ | Use a specific training log instead of the latest. |
| `--misclassified PATH` | _(auto: latest)_ | Use a specific misclassified JSON instead of the latest. |
| `--port PORT` | `8000` | vLLM server port. |
| `--model NAME` | `Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct` | vLLM model identifier. |
| `--seed N` | `42` | Random seed for image sampling (reproducibility). |
| `--no-vlm` | false | Skip all VLM calls — outputs stats and cluster data only. |

### V3-only flags

| Flag | Default | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `--no-rag` | false | Skip RAG index build/query (faster cold start for single-run analysis). |
| `--rebuild-rag` | false | Force rebuild of `rag_index.npz` even if a cached version exists. |
| `--rag-index PATH` | `reports/rag_index.npz` | Override the RAG index file path. |
| `--no-tool-trace` | false | Hide agent tool verification results from the rendered report. |

---

## Report Structure

All three versions write a self-contained Markdown file to the project root.
All images (sample thumbnails, cluster plots, distribution charts) are
**base64-embedded** — the report renders correctly with no external files.

### V1 — `ai_reasoning_summary.md`

```
1. Training Summary            accuracy / loss / epoch table
2. Failure Distribution        count + % per distortion type
3. Top Confusion Pairs         15 most common true → predicted pairs
4. Per-Image VLM Analysis      one subsection per sampled image
   ├── Distortion Artifacts
   ├── Surviving True-Class Features
   ├── What Misled the Model
   ├── Model Reasoning — Correct
   ├── Model Reasoning — Incorrect
   ├── Confidence Assessment
   └── Root Cause
5. Model Blind Spot Summary    root causes joined across images
6. Recommendations             priority-ranked augmentation suggestions
```

### V2 — `ai_reasoning_summary_v2.md`

```
1. Training Summary
2. Failure Distribution
3. Top Confusion Pairs
4. Per-Distortion Synthesised Analysis   one subsection per type
   ├── Stats (true classes, wrong predictions, avg confidence)
   ├── Images analysed (with embedded thumbnails)
   ├── Turn 1 — visual observations (labels hidden from model)
   └── Turn 2 — synthesised analysis (labels revealed)
       🔗 Shared Failure Pattern
       ⭕↔⚠️  Typical vs Outlier
       ❌ What Misled the Model
       📊 Confidence Assessment
       🎯 Root Cause
5. Model Blind Spot Summary    model-synthesised across images
6. Recommendations
```

### V3 — `ai_reasoning_summary_v3.md`

```
1. Training Summary
2. Failure Distribution        + embedded distribution bar chart
3. Top Confusion Pairs
   [cluster plots embedded here]
4. Per-Distortion Agent Analysis   one subsection per type
   ├── Stats
   ├── Images analysed (post-dedup, embedded thumbnails)
   ├── RAG Context — top-5 visually similar past failures
   ├── Turn 1 — visual observations
   ├── Verification Tool Results (epoch trend, confusion counts)
   └── Turn 2 — Pydantic-structured analysis
       🔗 Shared Failure Pattern
       ⭕↔⚠️  Typical vs Outlier
       ❌ What Misled the Model
       📊 Confidence Assessment
       🎯 Root Cause
       🗃️  RAG — Novel vs Known Pattern
5. Model Blind Spot Summary    agent-synthesised, tool-verified
6. Actionable Recommendations  LLM-generated from live failure data
```

---

## Troubleshooting

**`ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'langchain_openai'` or similar**
The venv is not active. Always activate before running:
```bash
source ~/vision_dev_project/venv_vision/bin/activate
```

**`Server 400 — max_tokens too large`**
The VLM server was started with `--max-model-len 2048`. Restart with
`--max-model-len 4096` — V2/V3 two-turn prompts need the extra headroom.

**Turn 2 response stored as `_raw` (no section headers in report)**
The VLM did not follow the structured output format. Usually caused by
a low `--max-model-len`. Raise it to `4096`. The raw response is still
shown verbatim — no data is lost.

**RAG index build is slow on first V3 run**
The first run encodes every image across all past misclassified JSONs.
With thousands of failures this takes 1–2 minutes.
Subsequent runs load the cached `reports/rag_index.npz` in under 1 second.
Use `--no-rag` if you only need the current run's analysis.

**Agent loops to max iterations with no structured output**
`AGENT_MAX_ITERATIONS` (default 2) in `v3/config.py` was reached.
The report renders with whatever hypothesis text was produced.
Raise the constant in `config.py` if the agent needs more iterations.

**VLM server timeout (never becomes ready after 5 min)**
All report scripts fall back to stats-only mode automatically — no crash.
Run `nvidia-smi` to check whether the GPU is already occupied before
starting the server.

**`distortion_report.json` has empty archetype lists**
Pass the misclassified `.json` file (not a folder) to
`--base-dir` in `distortion_diagnostic_report.py`. The script decodes
the base64 images from the JSON automatically.

**Only 1–2 images available for a distortion type**
All scripts handle any number of images ≥ 1. The typical/outlier
comparison is skipped when there are fewer than 2 samples.
